title: stenochlaena smithii
intro: Stenochlaena smithii, also known as the Black Fern or Marsh Fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Marattiaceae. It is native to Australia, New Zealand, and parts of the Pacific Islands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Stenochlaena smithii include treating fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions, and herbal preparations often involve infusions, decoctions, and tinctures made from the plant's leaves and rhizomes.
